qt: A/An _______ loop in PL/SQL ensures that at least one statement is executed before the loop terminates.
Loop without exit condition is a/an _____________ loop.
By using PL/SQL _______, you can repeatedly execute one or more statements over and over again.

DECLARE no NUMBER := 0; BEGIN FOR no IN 1 .. 5 LOOP DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE('Iteration : ' || no); CONTINUE WHEN no = 4 DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE('CONTINUE WHEN EXECUTE Iteration : ' || no); END LOOP; END; / Output for this piece of code :
op: Iteration # 1 Iteration # 2 Iteration # 3 CONTINUE WHEN EXECUTE Iteration: 4
Iteration # 1 Iteration # 3 CONTINUE WHEN EXECUTE Iteration: 4 Iteration # 5
Iteration # 1 Iteration # 2 Iteration # 3 CONTINUE WHEN EXECUTE Iteration: 4 Iteration # 5

qt: You can also use CONTINUE to terminate an ___________loop and continue immediately on to the next iteration of an outer loop’s body.
BEGIN WHILE lv_cnt_num <= 5 LOOP DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE(lv_cnt_num); lv_cnt_num := lv_cnt_num + 1; END LOOP; END; According to the code fragment above, how many times does the loop iterate?

1. BEGIN 2. FOR loop_index IN 1..5 3. LOOP 4. dbms_output.put_line(loop_index); 5. dbms_output.put_line('Before the Continue statement'); 6. CONTINUE; 7. dbms_output.put_line('After the Continue statement'); 8. END LOOP loop_index; 9. dbms_output.put_line('Loop has been terminated'); 10. END; 11. /
op: 1 Before the Continue statement 2 Before the Continue statement 3 Before the Continue statement
1 Before the Continue statement 2 Before the Continue statement 3 Before the Continue statement 4 Before the Continue statement 5 Before the Continue statement Loop has been terminated
4 Before the Continue statement 5 Before the Continue statement Loop has been terminated
1 Before the Continue statement 2 Before the Continue statement 3 Before the Continue statement 4 Before the Continue statement

qt: Which of the following is TRUE while using PL/SQL FOR Loop?
op: The counter variable is implicitly declared in the declaration section, so you do not need to declare it explicitly.
It is not necessary to explicitly increment the counter variable since it is incremented by 1
FOR loops can use EXIT WHEN and EXIT statements, but it isn't often used.

qt: PL/SQL Loops are also known as
Sequence of statements are executed in each iteration.___________ loop exit when condition is evaluated to false.
A ______ statement allows us to execute a statement or group of statements multiple times.
When you want to execute a series of statements repeatedly, you use the PL/SQL ____ loop.
What is the syntax of PL/SQL Loop?
op: LOOP Sequence of statements; END LOOP;
END LOOP; LOOP Sequence of statements;
END LOOP; Sequence of statements; LOOP
LOOP END LOOP; Sequence of statements;

qt: Which structure executes a sequence of statements repeatedly as long as a condition holds true?

DECLARE x NUMBER := 0; BEGIN LOOP -- After CONTINUE statement, control resumes here DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ('Inside loop: x = ' || TO_CHAR(x)); x := x + 1; IF x < 3 THEN CONTINUE; END IF; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ('Inside loop, after CONTINUE: x = ' || TO_CHAR(x)); EXIT WHEN x = 5; END LOOP; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE (' After loop: x = ' || TO_CHAR(x)); END; / Output for this piece of code :
op: Inside loop: x = 0 Inside loop: x = 1 Inside loop: x = 2 Inside loop, after CONTINUE: x = 3 Inside loop: x = 3 Inside loop, after CONTINUE: x = 4
Inside loop: x = 0 Inside loop: x = 1 Inside loop: x = 2 Inside loop, after CONTINUE: x = 3

Inside loop: x = 0 Inside loop: x = 1 Inside loop: x = 2 Inside loop, after CONTINUE: x = 3 Inside loop: x = 3 Inside loop, after CONTINUE: x = 4 Inside loop: x = 4 Inside loop, after CONTINUE: x = 5 After loop: x = 5
Inside loop: x = 0 Inside loop: x = 1 Inside loop: x = 2 Inside loop, after CONTINUE: x = 3 Inside loop: x = 3 Inside loop, after CONTINUE: x = 4

qt: Which of the following code fragments would not raise an error?
op: BEGIN FOR i IN 1..10 LOOP DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE(i); END LOOP; END;
BEGIN FOR i IN 1..10 LOOP DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE(c) END LOOP END;
BEGIN FOR i IN 1..10 LOOP DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE END LOOP END;
BEGIN FOR i IN 1..10 DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE(i); END LOOP; END;

qt: How many times does a WHILE Loop run?
Does PL/SQL allows using one loop inside another loop.
________ loop  is use to repeat the set of operations. Control variable is initialized with end value. Value is decreased every time  by one.
How many types of PL/SQL Loops are there?
____________ loop should be executed at least once. No direct implementation is provided in PL/SQL. It can be implemented using simple loop.
What is the datatype of the counter used in FOR Loop?
op: Any data type other than DATE

qt: Where is the control transferred while using a CONTINUE statement?
op: First statement after the end of the loop
Last iteration of the current loop
Next iteration of the current loop
First iteration of the current loop

qt: Label appear at the ________ of the LOOP statement.
What is the initial value of index for a reverse for loop?
When does a FOR Loop end?
op: When the index reaches the UPPER bound or is terminated early using EXIT statement
When lower bound is greater than UPPER bound or is terminated early using EXIT statement
Only when the index reaches the UPPER bound
When lower bound and upper bound are equal

qt: Loop within a loop is called _____________ loop.
Sequence of statements is enclosed between the LOOP and the END LOOP statements.
BEGIN FOR i IN 1 .. 5 LOOP IF i = 3 THEN CONTINUE; END IF; DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE('Iteration # ' || i); END LOOP; END; Output for this piece of code :
The counter in PL/SQL FOR Loop is by default incremented by _____________.
FOR i IN 1..tbl_roast.COUNT LOOP lv_tot_num := lv_tot_num + tbl_roast(i); END LOOP; In the above code fragment, which of the following holds the value of the current iteration number?
The label in the LOOP statement should be enclosed by?
Program will run indefinitely and will result in abnormal termination.
op: If there is no exit condition written
If variable is defined more than one time

qt: The set of rows the cursor holds is referred to as the __________.
A _____ is a pointer to this context area.
What is the maximum number of exception handlers processed before the PL/SQL block is exited, provided an exception occurs?
op: All exceptions that are referenced in the block

qt: What can the value of the INTO clause be while fetching data in an explicit cursor?
Which of the following statements is true about record types in Oracle 11g?
op: They are user-defined data types that group related fields
They are predefined data types provided by Oracle
They cannot be used as table columns.
They can only store character data

qt: ........................... cursor are declared by ORACLE for each UPDATE, DELETE and INSERT SQL commands.
PL/SQL controls the context area through a cursor.
Which of the false statements about opening and closing an explicit cursor?
op: Open statement identifies the result set and positions the cursor before the first row
An explicit cursor is closed with the CLOSE statement.
Optionally arguments can be passed to the open statement to open a cursor
A cursor once closed cannot be re-opened.

qt: Which of the following is not correct about Cursor?
op: Cursor is a named Private SQL area
SQL uses implicit Cursors to retrieve rows
Cursor is used for retrieving multiple rows

qt: Which of the following statements is true about propagating exceptions in Oracle 11g?
op: All exceptions must be handled within the code block where they are raised.
The RAISE_APPLICATION_ERROR procedure is used to explicitly propagate user-defined errors.
Exceptions are automatically propagated by default.
Propagated exceptions cannot be handled within the current code block.

qt: Which of the following statements is true about implicit cursors?
op: Implicit cursors are used in cursor for loops to handle data processing.
Implicit cursors are used for SQL statements that are not named.
. Developers should use implicit cursors with great care.
Implicit cursors are no longer a feature in Oracle.

qt: How many rows can a cursor hold?
How many types of cursors?
What is the syntax of an implicit cursor attribute ISOPEN?
Explicit cursors are programmer-defined cursors for gaining more control over the context area.
Where do you declare an explicit cursor in the PL/SQL language?
op: In the PL/SQL working storage section

qt: Programmers cannot control the _______ cursors and the information in it.
Which of the operations of a cursor checks for the validity of a SQL statement?
......................... cursors are declared and used by the user to process multiple row, returned by SELECT statement.
The Implicit cursors are created in order to process the ____ statements.
Which type of cursor does an implicit and explicit cursor belong to?
When is an implicit cursor used?
op: DDL statement is run and the statements are not associated with any explicit cursor.
SELECT/DML statement is run and the statements are not associated explicit cursor.
SELECT or DML statement is run and not associated with any cursor variable.
SELECT statement is run even if the statements are associated with explicit cursor.

qt: List the correct sequence of commands to process a set of records when using explicit cursors
What is the purpose of the %ROWTYPE attribute in Oracle 11g?
op: To check if a variable holds a record data type
To convert a record variable into a string
To automatically populate a record with data from a table row
To define a new record type based on an existing table

qt: Which of the following statements is true about cursors and records in Oracle 11g?
op: You cannot use record variables with FOR loops iterating through a cursor
Cursors directly store records, eliminating the need for record variables
Record variables are required to hold data fetched from a cursor
Cursors automatically close after fetching the first record

qt: Which of the following is correct syntax to open the Cursor?
Which of the following built-in packages is used for advanced exception handling in Oracle 11g(PL/SQL)?
What is the name of the cursor written and maintained by a database user?
BEGIN  FOR l_index IN 1 .. 10  LOOP  CONTINUE WHEN MOD (l_index, 2) = 0;  DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ('Loop index = ' || TO_CHAR (l_index));  END LOOP; END; / Output for this piece of code :
op: Loop index = 1 Loop index = 3 Loop index = 5 Loop index = 7 Loop index = 9
Loop index = 5 Loop index = 7 Loop index = 9

qt: BEGIN  FOR i IN 1 .. 5 LOOP  IF i = 3 THEN  CONTINUE;  END IF;  DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE('Iteration # ' || i);  END LOOP; END; Output for this piece of code :

DECLARE   x NUMBER := 0;  BEGIN   LOOP -- After CONTINUE statement, control resumes here   DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE ('Inside loop: x = ' || TO_CHAR(x));   x := x + 1;   IF x < 3 THEN   CONTINUE;   END IF;   DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE   ('Inside loop, after CONTINUE: x = ' || TO_CHAR(x));   EXIT WHEN x = 5;   END LOOP;     DBMS_OUTPUT.PUT_LINE (' After loop: x = ' || TO_CHAR(x));  END;  /   Output for this piece of code :

Inside loop: x = 0 Inside loop: x = 1 Inside loop: x = 2 Inside loop, after CONTINUE: x = 3 Inside loop: x = 3 Inside loop, after CONTINUE: x = 4
